exploration_notes: Kafka's Java-based service lifecycle is primarily implemented in the Connect framework and supplemented by Streams and server utilities. The Herder interface (Herder.java) defines the lifecycle contract with start()/stop() methods. AbstractHerder implements startServices() which starts Worker -> StatusBackingStore -> ConfigBackingStore, and stopServices() which stops them in reverse order (StatusBackingStore -> ConfigBackingStore -> Worker). Connect.java orchestrates the top-level lifecycle: sta
